title: Fernet keys should not be disclosed
description: Fernet keys are symmetric encryption keys used with Python's cryptography library for secure data encryption. These keys are 44-character URL-safe base64-encoded strings that represent 32 bytes of cryptographic material. When a Fernet key is hardcoded in source code or configuration files, it creates a critical security vulnerability as anyone with repository access can decrypt sensitive data protected by this key.
recommendation: Remove hardcoded Fernet keys from source code. Store Fernet keys in environment variables, use a secrets management service (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, Azure Key Vault), or use secure configuration management. Never commit Fernet keys to version control systems. Rotate any exposed keys immediately.
impact: Exposed Fernet keys allow unauthorized parties to decrypt sensitive data, bypass authentication, compromise data integrity, and gain unauthorized access to protected systems and user information.

checks.message: Fernet encryption key detected. Fernet keys are exactly 44 characters (43 URL-safe base64 chars + '=' padding) representing a 256-bit symmetric encryption key from Python's cryptography library. Exposing this key allows anyone to decrypt all data encrypted with it, including stored passwords, personal data, session tokens, and other sensitive information. Rotate the key immediately and re-encrypt all data that was protected by the exposed key.

Hardcoded Fernet key passed directly to Fernet constructor. This pattern directly exposes a 256-bit symmetric encryption key used to protect data at rest. All data encrypted with this key is now compromised. Generate a new key with Fernet.generate_key(), store it securely in an environment variable or secrets manager, and re-encrypt all affected data.
